reviews.review_title: Great fun for a free experience
reviews.review_description: In my opinion, the game looks and feels how I would expect for a garten of banban vr game however, there were a couple of issues. The menu was also better than expected, being able to look around the starting area with an entity ive never seen before that stares into your soul when moving through a limited space. I seen my character randomly jump in place, but now I believe I was about to no clip into the void for doing absolutely nothing but standing or walking. I think if the issue gets resolved, I could have even more fun without worrying about dropping through the map in the first few minutes.

reviews.review_title: That bird is so darn creepy! Great port!
reviews.review_description:
If you need help finding the eggs, check out my playthrough, but you'll be seeing the bird afterwards...

https://youtu.be/rY26jhiCwD8?si=NtYEuDkNidohTdRL

LITERALLY UNFINISHED SLOP!!
There's absolutely no reason to play this game for about 15 minutes and the game is over, it's so stupid. And not only this, there's ANOTHER GAME.

When will chapter 2 and chapter 3 come out?

I love this!

There is a golden, unspoken rule of VR: Do not attempt to move someone in a way without their consent (teleporting them/throwing them in a way/direction that isn't explained/sensical.)

Either by sheer incompetence (usual for EB's games) or by a brilliant 4D move that us *peasants* won't understand,...

This game sets its default way to move is a weird hybrid of Head Direction and Controller Direction. By that, You could point in one direction with your head, completly expecting to go that way, only for the game to decide to go a completly different way. No way I could move consistently enough to get to places *without* triggering this ""mechanic"" and yeeting my character completly away from my goal (or off a cliff into a death pit, which HAPPENED A LOT FOR ME, THANKS. MORE THEN I EXPECTED, YESSIR).

For the one hour that I played this, it genuinelly made Me Nauseous. I cannot tell you why, but this straight up is pure jank.

And except for the fact, that the Drone remote is also awful (as if, it's needlessly complicated to use), it is a janky remake of GoBB1 for VR systems.
If you need a "Proper" review of this, watch a Review on YT of the flatscreen version and take this into account. In my opinion, however, this game sucks as a PC release, and is even worse in VR. A pure greed-move and nothing else.

Exited for Garten of Banban 17: Banban sucks a Hollow One.
the floor kept disappearing

reviews.review_title: Good for a starter vr game
reviews.review_description: i like the game but i don’t understand when you jump on the chair things it dosen’t move you for some reason and i went out of the map when i turned my headset off and back on but other than that the game is really good if you want to experience garten of banban  in vr

I knew this was a dumb kids horror game so I went in with low expectations but this is HORRIBLE even for a kids game all models look like bad free sketchfab models plus being a new player I spent about 80% of my wasted time walking around confused why I couldn't get past the part I'm on and most of the time it was because something glitched and I had to get killed to refresh the map. Overall it would only be fun for a 6 year old and even then they wouldn't know what to do without help.
I literally gave the bird ALL ITS EGGS and it didnt give me the keycard. Not to mention you can phase your hand THROUGH ANYTHING (which allowed me to get the hammer early)
This game is a forgettable and unfinished piece of dog 💩

When i installed this for a video, i thought of it as a punishment. But, the shading and physics shocked me! Sure, it is buggy, but i only fell through the map once. It's alright. Not trash, not bad, not good, but just ok.

I don't know why, but this game gave me major chills and tingles. Opila bird was funny when you could trick-shot eggs in its mouth. The hidden desk key card  was dumb and I walked around smashing everything with my drone for nearly 20 minutes. Other than that scariest and most immersive horror game on my VR. Definitely recommend it to older kids because it would freak me out if I was near 8 and I am being chased by a shiny, long-necked bird, that you shove eggs down it's neck and it coughs up a key card. 😶

I have been a fan of the series ever since the first game launched and I watched Youtubers play it and I’ve grown with the series and now that the series is ending on steam and consoles. I got yard for my birthday in December and it’s only like nine days after or 10 days after and it’s on launch day right now￼ then I saw it and downloaded it before it was going to have a price so far free hopes this helped btw no lag would recommend it thank you for your time bye, silly little Mr crab

it's really fun but it's kinda weird that when you're traveling over the ball pit it teleports you instead of getting on the sofa and going over there.
also it's kind of hard to access the backpack.
but anyways i love the game and hopefully euphoric brothers continue to add the sequels to vr

reviews.review_title: Really good game, more chapters please
reviews.review_description: This game is super and scary and its a very hood game for no money but its also a very short game. Please bring more chapters to vr

The game was fun, but everytime I try and use the drone to break the yellow glass so I can get the hammer the drone breaks the floor as well and I fall out of the map, then my game crashes, I also figured out if done properly you can just straight up launch out of the map with this glitch if you use your hands and bounce off a certain wall

The movement sucks, when I died my game crashed, and my progress was gone when I relaunched it. The few good things included was the fact that this version is actually optimized, B I R D F U N N Y, and it is short so I can easily get my progress back. But banban 2 is almost an hour, so if its vr port has the same issue, that will actually be a problem.

The original game is bad at best, but in my opinion, a VR adaptation was a good decision. It's honestly enjoyable and a step up from the original, I feel like the original game is overhated but if you have played the first releases then this VR version is significantly better, and for free it's worth the try 👍

I really like how being in vr changes the immersion, seeing opilia now is way scarier now that it feels like she is really going for you,the backpack is also an awesome adition, the only complaint i have is how you cant move during jump, it doesnt change anything in this game but in future ones it could complicated considering there could be parkour
